Description:
*As a* Data Operations Analyst, *I want to* automatically connect to the FTP server and download the daily claims CSV file, *so that* I don't have to manually retrieve files and can ensure timely data processing.

*Acceptance Criteria:*
1. System connects to FTP server daily at a scheduled time (e.g., 6:00 AM)
2. System downloads the latest claims CSV matching the expected filename pattern
3. Download is logged with timestamp, file size, and status (success/failure)
4. Alert is sent to team if download fails or file is not available by deadline
5. Downloaded file is stored in a designated staging folder with date-stamped naming

*Definition of Done:*
1. All acceptance criteria verified by PO
2. Code reviewed and merged to main branch
3. Unit tests written and passing
4. Tested with real or representative sample claims file
5. Edge cases handled (empty file, missing tab, FTP timeout, null values)
6. Configuration steps documented (FTP creds, schema config, distribution list)
7. No critical bugs open against the story
8. PO demo/review completed and story accepted in Jira

*As a* Data Operations Analyst, *I want to* receive immediate notification if the FTP download fails, *so that* I can take manual corrective action before downstream processes are impacted.

*Acceptance Criteria:*
1. System retries connection up to 3 times before marking as failed
2. Email/Slack alert is sent on failure with error details
3. Failure is logged in audit trail
4. System supports manual trigger for re-download

1. All acceptance criteria verified by PO
2. Code reviewed and merged to main branch

5. Edge cases handled (empty file, missing tab, FTP timeout, null values)
6. Configuration steps documented (FTP creds, schema config, distribution list)
7. No critical bugs open against the story
8. PO demo/review completed and story accepted in Jira

*As a* Data Quality Analyst, *I want to* automatically verify all required tabs are present in the daily file, *so that* I can catch incomplete files before they corrupt downstream reports.

*Acceptance Criteria:*
1. System checks for all expected tabs defined in a configuration list
2. Missing tabs trigger a validation failure with specific tab names listed
3. Validation report is generated with pass/fail status per tab
4. File is quarantined (not processed further) if critical tabs are absent
5. Stakeholder alert sent with validation summary

*Definition of Done:*
1. All acceptance criteria verified by PO
2. Code reviewed and merged to main branch
3. Unit tests written and passing
4. Tested with real or representative sample claims file
5. Edge cases handled (empty file, missing tab, FTP timeout, null values)
6. Configuration steps documented (FTP creds, schema config, distribution list)
7. No critical bugs open against the story
8. PO demo/review completed and story accepted in Jira

*As a* Data Quality Analyst, *I want to* verify column headers, data types, and field formats match the expected schema, *so that* I can detect vendor-side changes or errors before they propagate into reports.

*Acceptance Criteria:*
1. System validates column names match expected schema per tab
2. Date fields are checked for correct format (e.g., MM/DD/YYYY)
3. Numeric fields are validated as non-negative and within expected range
4. Row count is compared against prior day — flag if >20% variance
5. Validation results logged with row-level error details where applicable

*Definition of Done:*
1. All acceptance criteria verified by PO
2. Code reviewed and merged to main branch
3. Unit tests written and passing
4. Tested with real or representative sample claims file
5. Edge cases handled (empty file, missing tab, FTP timeout, null values)
6. Configuration steps documented (FTP creds, schema config, distribution list)
7. No critical bugs open against the story
8. PO demo/review completed and story accepted in Jira

*As a* Data Quality Analyst, *I want to* identify null or blank values in required fields, *so that* I can ensure data integrity before analysis begins.

*Acceptance Criteria:*
1. Required fields are defined in configuration
2. System flags rows where required fields are null or blank
3. Summary count of null violations is included in validation report
4. Threshold-based rule: file fails if >5% of rows have critical null values

Summary: CSV to Formatted Excel Workbook Conversion
Description:
*As a* Data Operations Analyst, *I want to* automatically convert the validated CSV file into a formatted Excel workbook, *so that* stakeholders receive a polished, ready-to-use file instead of raw CSV.

*Acceptance Criteria:*
1. Each data tab from the CSV maps to a corresponding Excel sheet
2. File is saved as .xlsx with a date-stamped filename
3. Headers are bold, frozen, and styled with brand color
4. All columns are auto-fitted to content width
5. Alternating row shading applied for readability

*As a* Data Operations Analyst, *I want to* apply consistent formatting rules across all data tabs, *so that* reports are immediately readable and professional without manual cleanup.

*Acceptance Criteria:*

3. Numeric columns right-aligned; text columns left-aligned
4. Conditional formatting highlights values exceeding defined thresholds

*As a* Healthcare Operations Manager, *I want to* view a single-page KPI dashboard within the Excel workbook, *so that* I can monitor performance at a glance without navigating multiple tabs.

Summary: Trend Analysis & Anomaly Flagging on Dashboard
Description:
*As a* Healthcare Operations Manager, *I want to* see trend lines and automatic anomaly flags on the dashboard, *so that* I can take action on outliers without waiting for manual analysis.

*Acceptance Criteria:*
1. 7-day and 30-day trend sparklines shown beside each KPI
2. Anomaly detection flags days where volume deviates >2 standard deviations from mean
3. Flagged anomalies annotated on trend charts
4. Summary note auto-generated describing top changes from prior period
